channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The latest twist in the embarrassing WordPress saga involves pineapple pizza
item.description: The drama at WordPress has taken a number of twists and turns in the months since Matt Mullenweg, founder and CEO of for-profit WordPress host Automattic, shut off access to WP Engine, a third-party platform that many users deploy to host their websites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ozy Media founder Carlos Watson sentenced to 10 years
item.description: Carlos Watson, founder of the defunct digital media company Ozy Media, was sentenced to 10 years in prison for lying to investors about the startup's finances.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: DoD Edges Toward Goal of Ubiquitous Connectivity
item.description: The U.S. military is undergoing a communications revolution, driven by changes in policy and culture that are pushing the armed services to embrace commercial technologies and ubiquitous connectivity.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents
item.description: The draft national cyber incident response plan comes eight years after the last update, and more than six years after the creation of CISA itself. The post CISA lays out how agencies, industry should respond to major cyber incidents first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he has a 'warm spot' in his heart for TikTok as app faces ban
item.description: President-elect Trump said Monday that he has a “warm spot” in his heart for TikTok as the popular social media app faces down a potential ban next month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Harris campaign aide accuses Musk of throttling content before election
item.description: A campaign aide to Vice President Harris accused tech billionaire Elon Musk of throttling content on his social platform X ahead of the 2024 election to benefit President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Eddie AI is one of the best video editing tools out there
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: How to factory reset your Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how to securely back up and factory reset your Android to protect your privacy and data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Google’s Agentspace will put AI agents in the hands of workers
item.description: Google has unveiled an AI agent builder tool designed to automate repititive tasks and help workers find information held across their organization faster. AI agents have become a major focus for software vendors in recent months, including Atlassian, Microsoft, Salesforce, and numerous others.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Think you're safe? Identity theft could wipe out your entire life’s savings
item.description: A former Colgate-Palmolive employee was shocked to discover $750,000 had been drained from her 401(k) account. "CyberGuy" offers tips on how to prevent identity theft.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 18 indispensable Android travel apps
item.description: For all the ways travel’s evolved over the years, one thing has remained maddeningly steadfast: Moving from one place to another is almost always a hassle. There’s endless room for inconvenience and error, and a journey rarely goes according to plan.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chicago flunks cloud economics
item.description: The city of Chicago has devised a brilliant plan to jack up its cloud computing tax from 9% to 11%. As someone who has worked in tech for decades, I’ve seen this movie before, and (spoiler alert) it didn’t have a happy ending. This is not a diatribe against taxes; taxes are not the issue here.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Smart cities in action: Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV project sets a new standard
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: From surveillance to safety: How Kazakhstan’s Carpet CCTV is reshaping security
item.description: In a world where technology increasingly shapes how cities manage safety and security, Kazakhstan’s Ministry of Internal Affairs is leading the way with its groundbreaking “Carpet CCTV” project.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Federal judge slaps down Automattic, granting temporary injunction to WP Engine in ongoing WordPress squabble
item.description: The battle between WordPress owner Automattic and WP Engine seemingly struck US federal Judge Araceli Martinez-Olguin as rather one-sided, as she ruled against Automattic on Tuesday and granted WP Engine the preliminary injunction it sought.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
